channel.title: Comments for page "The Real Adventures in Capitalism"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "The Real Adventures in Capitalism"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is that a LISA reference under 212?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Incorrect on all counts, I am afraid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So in conclusion of what everyone is reading; 90% of all 3000 SCP's are dead/ banished form fate, 30% of the Foundation with humanity is dead or descended more below what any creature of creation CAN do(implying humanity are possibly more ancient than creation), 239 is confirmed to be the new All-Mighty(as the child if you read properly in Abrahamic lore) with 343's fate to be decided by another power that is older BUT weaker than him, Scarlet King and his Brides still exist but have no power to live thus believed themselves dead, and lastly the 4th-wall 001 is crowned as its steed for Wondertainment's newest and first creation…his child.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: [deleted]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As a headcanon? I really dislike it. But ah well, that's why I made my own Wondertainment canon. But as a piece of fiction? I really like it. I can feel the "I had too many ideas and I couldn't write them all but I had to get them out somehow" in this tale and I relate so hard. A joy to read. Have your +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I…I am so confused. I ran into Assistance for the Boss Lady and was like "oh boy! a funny little tale! and it's in chapters!" then around the 3rd chapter I got completely lost. Now I'm even more lost, but I feel moved somehow, even though i understood none of what was typed.

I'm still so confused I can't even ask any question other than "WHAT?!" and maybe "WHY?!". What was that? What did it all mean? Some of the comments are mentioning another canon I'm not familiar with, which I imagine would give me more insight into whatever the devil this was.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I keep reading SD in John Delancey's voice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Does the heart good to hear that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
IT'S ABOUT GODDAMN TIME.

Honestly, this might be the most Djoric piece that has ever been Djoric'd into existence, from the slightly off-kilter style to the intense, quasi-religious imagery. It's long, it's unabashed in its style, and it gives absolutely zero fucks.

Thankfully, I love that, so it's a hearty +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Glad you like it! This is the last of it, I'm afraid. We're all wrapped up. When my eyes were far bigger than my stomach and babbling about the whole Verse of an Endless Song thing, this was more or less the end goal of that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A better post than I made before:

I can understand why some readers who may not be super familiar with Adventures in Capitalism and the ETDP canons might find this a bit long or hard to follow. But those two canons are among my favorite stories anywhere on the site, and I absolutely loved this.

It was wonderful to see these two amazing plot arcs drawn together. Before reading this I'd never really considered how they related to each other, chronologically. You've given a fantastic amount of extra context and backstory to everything that happened in those arcs, I have to re-read them now.

Is this just a one-off, or can I hope for more additions to these canons (or any others) in the future?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wish there was a movie, believe me. A full series of 26 tales is a great deal more than what I could manage for it, alas.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is amazing!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the content, but it comes across as a little boring reading it.

It's like reading an IMDB plot summary when you could just watch the movie.

Novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Oh man, is that title like the Real Ghostbusters or something?"

“The Real Adventures in Capitalism” is a memetic condition consisting of spontaneously-occurring false memories of an animated television show of the same name.

Instant upvote.

And then I tried to upvote again at the end. :V Enjoy your +2.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was pretty exhausting to write, so I definitely understand. The backload of a lot of the length on the last three episodes is the result of coming up with that stuff years ago and then filling in the rest more recently.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like how you inspire strong emotions and reactions in basically everything you write. So I have a simple explanation for why I downvoted this.

It was so long that it dragged for me and without any purpose or aim it felt exhausting to read. Coupled with the overly flowery language at points, it was a struggle. A good foundation but I don't like the execution.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why haven't I heard of this?! This is really something, that's for sure. Definitely going to look at all of these in order to get better understanding of this.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you've got any burning questions in need of clarification, I can clear those up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I did not understand half the references.

I don't think I understood half the names.

I don't recognize half the cross links.

But I'll be damned if this didn't put a whole smile on my face. Well done.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Jesus. FUCKING. CHRIST.
I don't understand what just happened, but I think I love it.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I remember catching this a long time ago as a skip.

Then I remembered it as a draft.

I'm really glad to see this posted. I really enjoyed this, and I'm glad to see it's finally OVA!

+1

item.title: DON'T CALL IT A COMEBACK
item.content:encoded:
It’s a been a long, long time. But, after four and a half years, it is finished. It’s all finished.

Many thanks to kaktus and Hippo, who gave feedback in chat.

If anyone is inspired to make fanart of the show, say the word and it shall be added to the page proper.

Playing You Say Run / Jet Set Run at max volume during the final episode is completely appropriate.

For those unfamiliar (and no doubt many are), the links lead to additional context.

If you have further questions, ask away, I can answer them all.

There is absolutely a lost OVA titled "Grillin' Shit with Mary-Ann".

A-dee-badee-b’dee dat’s all, folks!
